name: After-Dinner Mint Brownies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease a 15 ½" x 10 ½" jelly-roll pan. In a heavy 4-quart saucepan over low heat, melt the unsweetened chocolate squares until smooth, stirring frequently.
2. In a large bowl, beat sugar, corn syrup, vanilla, and 1 cup butter or margarine with a mixer on low speed until blended. Increase to high speed; beat until light and fluffy. Reduce to low; add flour, salt, and eggs, beating until well mixed, scraping the bowl frequently.
3. Increase speed to high; beat in melted chocolate until smooth. Stir in walnuts. Spread batter evenly in the pan. Bake 40-45 minutes until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. Cool brownies on a wire rack.
4. Prepare mint butter-cream frosting: In a large bowl, beat confectioner's sugar, milk, peppermint extract, and 6 tablespoons butter until smooth. Stir in enough green food coloring to achieve a pleasing green color. Spread frosting evenly over cooled brownies. Refrigerate while preparing Chocolate Glaze.
5. Prepare Chocolate Glaze; spread it over the frosting to completely cover the top. Refrigerate until set for about 1 hour. Cut brownies lengthwise into 3 strips, then crosswise into 15 bars. Makes 45 bars, each containing approximately 245 calories.
